title: Add configurable call event sampling to bound SQLite write amplification

requirements.acceptance: With default config, every valid call event that is currently persisted remains persisted and existing tests/contracts stay compatible.
Config supports an explicit sampling mode/rate with validation, documented bounds, and no external dependencies.
last_tps, avg_tps, peak_tps, total tokens, and duration remain based on every valid hook event regardless of historical sampling.
Sampling decision uses O(1) in-memory logic and does not require extra SQLite reads before deciding whether to write an event row.
Historical export/API/contract metadata can report sampling mode/rate and whether returned call-event history may be incomplete.
Diagnostics or operational metrics expose how many event rows were skipped due to sampling without leaking sensitive identifiers.
Tests cover disabled/default behavior, rate validation, always/never sample boundaries, deterministic decisions, and metadata output.

success_criteria: Default configuration persists complete historical call-event rows as before.
Configured sampling reduces persisted historical rows while keeping aggregate session stats exact.
Sampling policy is documented in API/export/contract metadata.
Invalid sampling configuration fails predictably with clear validation errors.
No regressions in privacy redaction, API response compatibility, or plugin registration.
constraints: Do not implement during bead-production phase.
Do not duplicate existing persistence, export, privacy, health, rate-limiting, or batch-stats beads.
Do not run /plan in this phase.
